iti22:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti22:2.1: “Monks, don’t be afraid of acts of merit.
iti22:2.2: This is a synonym for what is blissful, desirable, pleasing, endearing, charming–i.e., acts of merit.
iti22:2.3:
I directly know that, having long performed meritorious deeds,
I long experienced desirable, pleasing, endearing, charming results.
iti22:2.4:
Having developed a mind of good will for seven years,
then for seven eons of contraction & expansion I didn’t return to this world.
iti22:2.5: Whenever the eon was contracting, I entered the [realm of] Radiance.
iti22:2.6: Whenever the eon was expanding, I reappeared in an empty Brahma-abode.
iti22:3.1: There I was Brahma, the Great Brahma, the Unconquered Conqueror, Total Seer, Wielder of Power.
iti22:3.2: Then for thirty-six times I was Sakka, ruler of the gods.
iti22:3.3:
For many hundreds of times I was a king, a wheel-turning emperor,
a righteous king of Dhamma, conqueror of the four corners of the earth,
maintaining stable control over the countryside,
endowed with the seven treasures –to say nothing of the times I was a local king.
iti22:4.2: ‘Of what action of mine is this the fruit, of what action the result, that I now have such great power & might?’
iti22:4.3: Then the thought occurred to me,
iti22:4.4:
‘This is the fruit of my three [types of] action,
the result of three types of action, that I now have such great power & might:
iti22:5.2: that yield the foremost profit of bliss–
iti22:5.3: develop generosity, a life in tune,
iti22:6.2: three things that bring about bliss,
iti22:6.3: the wise reappear in a world of bliss unalloyed.
